event: delivered by emergency cesarean delivery at 31 weeks of pregnancy
the infant was the product of a multiple pregnancy
Apgar of 4 in the first minute of life
Apgar of 8 in the fifth minute of life
mild respiratory distress (after first newborn resuscitation)
admitted to the Neonatal Intensive Care Unit (NICU) for further treatment
heart rate was constant at 167 beats per min
normal blood count, electrolytes, and liver function on admission
respiratory distress syndrome ground-glass opacity on X-ray
mild respiratory acidosis on blood gas
trophic feeding of breast milk started
a raised C-reactive protein level of 70 mg/L
antibiotics escalated to meropenem and vancomycin
slight abdominal distension and leftover milk
bilirubin, AST, and ALT levels rose
TSH, T3, T4, and cortisol not significant
negative for galactosemia and tyrosinemia type 1
negative for a wide range of metabolic disorders
caffeine (5 mg/kg/day) and hemoglobin optimization attempted
required 3 min of aggressive cardio-respiratory resuscitation
final diagnosis of Alagille syndrome 2 (ALGS2), from whole exome sequencing
